Your Personal Morning Dashboard

James' Morning Briefing

Saturday, April 25, 2026
Top Story

Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine, Dnipro hit hardest

Russian drone and missile strikes on the Ukrainian city of Dnipro killed at least seven people. Four died in a strike on a residential building. Over 20 wounded including a nine-year-old child and two police officers.

Read Full Story →

World News

Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine, Dnipro hit hardest

Russian drone and missile strikes on the Ukrainian city of Dnipro killed at least seven people. Four died in a strike on a residential building. Over 20 wounded including a nine-year-old child and two police officers.

BBC News · Europe · Read more

Trump dispatches Witkoff and Kushner to Pakistan for Iran peace talks

US envoy Steve Witkoff and Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner traveled to Pakistan for new talks with Iran's foreign minister. Trump extended a three-week ceasefire to allow more time for negotiations.

Associated Press · Middle East · Read more

NATO says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain

An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish NATO allies over perceived lack of support for Iran war. NATO officials push back at reported US threat to Spain.

BBC News · World · Read more

Explosions and gunfire as armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali

Witnesses report clashes in the centre and north of Mali in what has been described as the largest jihadist attack in years.

BBC News · Africa · Read more

Seattle News

Seattle Police Investigate Crash Involving Truck and Cyclists

Seattle police are investigating a crash that involved a truck and two cyclists on a Lime bike Monday morning.

FOX 13 Seattle · Read more

70-Year-Old Traffic Flagger Hospitalized After Capitol Hill Incident

A 70-year-old traffic flagger was hospitalized after being struck by a dump truck on Capitol Hill on Friday morning, according to Seattle police.

KOMO News · Read more

City Begins Safety Improvements at Gas Works Park

The city has begun safety improvements at Gas Works Park following recent concerns about the popular waterfront destination.

KIRO 7 · Read more

Tensions Flare Over Adams Elementary School Principal Appointment

Tensions flared more than once this week over the newly appointed principal of Seattle's Adams Elementary School, located in Ballard.

MyNorthwest · Read more

Technology

Google Commits to Invest Up to $40 Billion in Anthropic

Google has committed to invest as much as $40 billion in Anthropic as the AI startup accelerates its business behind the explosive growth of Claude Code. This represents one of the largest AI investments to date.

The New York Times · News · Read more

AI Arms Race Accelerates With New Models from OpenAI, DeepSeek and Anthropic

New AI model releases include DeepSeek's V4, OpenAI's GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7. The rapid pace of releases highlights the intensifying competition in the AI space.

CNET · News · Read more

Google Puts AI Agents at Heart of Enterprise Money-Making Push

Alphabet is deepening its push into enterprise software, signaling that AI agents are a linchpin of its strategy to monetize artificial intelligence at Google Cloud.

Reuters · News · Read more

Google's Internal Struggle is Handing the AI Coding Race to Anthropic and OpenAI

Some teams at DeepMind, including those working on Gemini, reportedly use Claude Code internally. Google is now releasing new AI agents to challenge OpenAI and Anthropic in the coding assistant market.

Los Angeles Times · News · Read more
